other_models: HAR, HAR-J, HAR-CJ, LogHAR, MHAR, ridge, lasso, elastic net, regression tree, random forest, gradient boosting, neural network, ensemble
winner_advantage_quote: ML is competitive and beats the HAR lineage; random forest and neural networks are preferred among ML algorithms and significantly improve prediction relative to HAR
notes: Tier-1 horse race; uses DM (one-sided) and Hansen-Lunde-Nason MCS; multiple winners across categories (best linear=elastic net 8.4% MSE reduction; best tree=RF; best ML=NN/RF; best HAR=LogHAR)

other_models: FNN, ENN, JNN, LSTM, LSTMX, NARX, GARCH-family, ARMA, stochastic vol
winner_advantage_quote: recurrent neural networks are able to outperform all the traditional econometric methods; the SSM contained only the NARX model which provided the best overall performance in terms of accuracy
notes: Most-cited paper in our sample (c=105); paper applies Hansen MCS test; multi-step horizon: NARX is the only SSM survivor

headline_winner: Random forest + HAR (with purified IV feature)
other_models: HAR-RV, random forest, purified implied volatility feature
winner_advantage_quote: random forests algorithm to improve the forecasting of realised volatility... based on the HAR model framework with the inclusion of the purified implied volatility
notes: Builds RF on top of HAR features; no formal sig test mentioned in abstract

other_models: BSS process, FSV (Comte-Renault), RFSV (Gatheral et al.)
winner_advantage_quote: introduce a class of continuous-time models of volatility incorporating both roughness and persistence
notes: Methodological paper introducing BSS class; less of a horse race

other_models: HAR, HARQ, HARK (proposed), SHAR, SHARK
winner_advantage_quote: HARK is always lower than the benchmark models
notes: Strong horse-race element comparing HAR variants; informal sig (no MCS/DM/SPA in abstract)

other_models: HAR-RV, HAR-CJ, hierarchical reconciliation methods (bottom-up, regression-based)
winner_advantage_quote: the use of intraday hierarchies improves the forecast accuracy
notes: Methodological paper; horse race against HAR baselines

other_models: two-scale RV, multi-scale RV, realized kernel, pre-averaging RV, QML, price-duration estimator (proposed)
winner_advantage_quote: price duration estimators can extract relevant information from high-frequency data better and produce smaller errors
notes: Methodological focus on volatility ESTIMATION (not just forecast); horse race against alternative QV estimators

other_models: HAR + 4 baseline vol models, residual-type HAR-SVR, weight-type HAR-SVR
winner_advantage_quote: according to the model confidence set test, the weight-type model outperforms the HAR model and the residual-type HAR-SVR model
notes: Explicit MCS test outcome stated in abstract; clearest horse race in MDPI cohort

other_models: ARIMA, SVR, hybrid ARIMA-SVR
winner_advantage_quote: this paper proposes using a hybrid model benefiting from ARIMA and support vector regression... to forecast
notes: Hybrid wins over each parent model; no formal sig test

other_models: GARCH, EWMA, RNN
winner_advantage_quote: the RNN outperforms GARCH and EWMA in average forecasting performance. However... less efficient in capturing extreme events
notes: Mixed verdict: RNN best in MSE, GARCH/EWMA better in VaR; explicitly contradicts ML-always-better view

other_models: GARCH-MIDAS variants (with geopolitical risk + production index + economic expectations), GARCH-MIDAS-LSTM
winner_advantage_quote: significant root mean squared error reduction with the novel GARCH-MIDAS-LSTM model over GARCH-MIDAS models. Percentage decline in root mean squared errors for forecasts are between 39% to 95%
notes: Large effect size (39-95% RMSE reduction) but no formal sig test in abstract

other_models: Bayesian SV, GARCH variants
winner_advantage_quote: the SV model performs better than the GARCH family models. Moreover the forecasting errors of the SV model... tend to be more accurate as forecast time horizons are longer
notes: 9 cryptocurrencies tested; SV beats GARCH especially at longer horizons

other_models: multiple GARCH models, LSTM, hybrid GARCH-LSTM
winner_advantage_quote: the hybrid model significantly improves value-at-risk (VaR) prediction performance compared to traditional GARCH models
notes: Focus on VaR not pure vol forecast; hybrid wins

other_models: GARCH, LSTM, GRU, BiLSTM (single/double/triple layer), hybrid combinations
winner_advantage_quote: such hybrid models can generate accurate forecasts of Bitcoin's price volatility
notes: Vague winner; hybrid wins but specific architecture not pinpointed

other_models: GARCH, GJR-GARCH (with NIG distribution), ANN, ARIMA
winner_advantage_quote: For some cryptocurrencies the ANN models perform better than traditional ARIMA models
notes: Mixed: ANN beats ARIMA for SOME cryptocurrencies, not all

other_models: Bayesian VAR, GARCH, stochastic volatility (Gaussian errors), stochastic vol (Student-t errors)
winner_advantage_quote: stochastic volatility is significantly outperforming the benchmark of VAR in both point and density forecasting; the student-t distribution is shown to outperform the standard normal approach
notes: Density forecasting evaluation; clear winner stated; uses 'significantly' but no formal test specified

other_models: realized backward-looking vol, GARCH, GARCH-NN hybrid
winner_advantage_quote: Our solution provides a more accurate volatility estimation
notes: VolTarget strategy paper; less of a pure forecasting horse race

other_models: TCN with attention, TCN without attention, GARCH, HAR-RV, ARFIMA, LSTM with attention
winner_advantage_quote: the prediction accuracy of the TCN model with investor attention is better than those of the TCN model without investor attention, the traditional econometric model... and the LSTM model with investor attention
notes: Multi-model horse race; clear winner; large model lineup

other_models: GARCH-MIDAS, GARCH-MIDAS-MV (with macro vars), GARCH-MIDAS-EPU, CNN-BiLSTM-Attention
winner_advantage_quote: the CNN-BiLSTM-Attention model yields the most accurate forecasts compared to the benchmark models
notes: Clear winner; benchmarks include both classical (GARCH-MIDAS) and DL

other_models: GARCH-Normal-VaR, GARCH-t-VaR, parametric VaR, SVR-GARCH-KDE
winner_advantage_quote: VaR estimates based on linear and parametric models can lead to biased results
notes: VaR-focused; SVR-GARCH-KDE proposed as nonparametric alternative

other_models: ARIMA, dilated CNN (single-stock training), dilated CNN (transfer learning)
winner_advantage_quote: its performance turns out to be much better in terms of prediction error
notes: Transfer-learning angle; clear winner against own non-transfer baseline

other_models: matrix-HAR, multivariate GARCH, geometric DL (Stiefel manifold)
winner_advantage_quote: our method outperforms traditional approaches in terms of predictive accuracy
notes: Realized covariance forecasting (not univariate vol); methodological paper

other_models: linear PCA, linear ICA, average measure, autoencoder, all combined with Realized GARCH
winner_advantage_quote: the superior effectiveness of the proposed model in one-step-ahead rolling volatility forecasting
notes: Master's thesis-level paper; nonlinear (autoencoder) > linear (PCA/ICA)

other_models: GARCH, GCN, GAT, Temporal GAT (combined GCN+GAT)
winner_advantage_quote: the Temporal GAT outperforms traditional GARCH models and other machine learning methods particularly in short- to mid-term forecasts
notes: 8 global indices; clear winner stated

other_models: GARCH, LSTM, GINN (their hybrid)
winner_advantage_quote: GINN showed superior out-of-sample prediction performance in terms of the Coefficient of Determination (R²), Mean Squared Error (MSE), and Mean Absolute Error (MAE)
notes: Hybrid wins on all 3 metrics; clear horse race

other_models: GARCH, LSTM, hybrid LSTM-GARCH, hybrid LSTM-GARCH+VIX
winner_advantage_quote: machine learning approaches particularly the hybrid LSTM models significantly outperform the traditional GARCH model. Including the VIX index in the hybrid model further enhances its forecasting ability
notes: 4-model horse race; clear monotone ranking

headline_winner: GARCH (rolling) for GBP/USD; GARCH+OLS+IV for EUR/GBP
other_models: EWMA, GARCH variants, IV models, OLS+IV combinations
winner_advantage_quote: for the GBP/USD pair, the most accurate volatility forecasts stem from the utilization of GARCH models employing a rolling window methodology... for the EUR/GBP pair optimal forecasts are derived from GARCH models and Ordinary Least Squares models incorporating implied volatility
notes: Two FX pairs with different winners; unusual nuanced result

other_models: classical GARCH variants, pipeline-style GARCH+NN hybrids, Transformer, GARCH-LSTM, GARCH-GRU
winner_advantage_quote: both models consistently outperform classical GARCH specifications, pipeline-style hybrids, and neural baselines such as the Transformer; GARCH-GRU achieves the strongest accuracy-efficiency tradeoff
notes: Multi-metric multi-baseline horse race; GARCH-GRU narrowly beats GARCH-LSTM

other_models: BEKK, DCC, LSTM-BEKK
winner_advantage_quote: the LSTM-BEKK model achieves superior performance in terms of out-of-sample portfolio risk forecast
notes: Multivariate volatility (not univariate); LSTM-augmented BEKK wins

headline_winner: Coefficient-based soft regime algorithm with HAR
other_models: baseline AR, soft Markov switching, spectral clustering with XGBoost, coefficient-based soft regime
winner_advantage_quote: The coefficient-based clustering algorithm outperformed all other models including the baseline autoregressive model during all time periods
notes: Regime-switching focus; coefficient-based clustering wins consistently
